$description: Accessibility constraints as PINNED, content-addressed objects. A constraint is a typed object (exactly like a color value): the standard, dated version, criterion, level, and required ratio. A declaration's a11y claim REFERENCES one by its content address — it never inlines a bare number. Changing the WCAG version yields a different object (different address), so a claim is provably against a specific, dated standard. Grounded: WCAG 2.2 (REC 2023-10-05).

$value.name: Use of Color
$value.rule: color must not be the ONLY visual means of conveying information — a distinction must also survive on a non-hue channel. Lightness is the CVD-robust axis (color-vision deficiency, ~1/12 men, collapses hue channels but leaves lightness intact), so a pair distinguished by lightness passes by construction; a pair distinguished only by hue along a confusable axis (e.g. red vs green at equal lightness) fails. Check: near-dupe under CVD simulation (Brettel/Viénot) must exceed the JND, OR the pair must differ in lightness.
